contributingFactors: log-collector Deployment: infinite loop writing 256KB random files to emptyDir every 5s (~3 MB/min, ~180 MB/hr) with no rotation or cleanup — files are never deleted
nginx-cache: init container pre-wrote ~200 MB of random files to emptyDir at startup (one-time burst, now static)
postgres-emptydir: PostgreSQL database stored on emptyDir (/dev/vda4), grows with DB usage
redis-scratch: session-loader sidecar wrote ~100 MB burst to emptyDir; Redis AOF persistence ongoing on same volume
All emptyDir volumes back to the same /dev/vda4 XFS partition (node /var filesystem)

Node's /var filesystem at only 36.8% free (~23.5 GiB) and declining — Prometheus range data confirms continuous downward trend from 37.05% to 36.83% over ~2 hours with accelerated drops at pod deployment time
Kubelet restarted at 19:30:54Z (NodeHasNoDiskPressure event confirms a clean start), but disk consumption resumes immediately with all 4 pods running
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The demo-diskpressure namespace contains four deliberately disk-consuming workloads on node dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com, led by the log-collector Deployment running an unbounded 256KB write loop every 5 seconds with no log rotation, continuously draining the node's XFS /var filesystem (currently 36.8% free of 63.2 GiB).

# Pod Summary: Namespace `demo-diskpressure`

## Overview
- **Namespace:** `demo-diskpressure`
- **Node:** `dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com` (all pods)
- **Total Pods:** 4, all in **Running** phase
- **QoS Class:** Burstable (all pods)
- **Created:** 2026-04-27 ~19:31:20Z

---

## ⚠️ Incident-Relevant Observations

### Disk Pressure Pattern — **All pods are deliberately writing large amounts of data to emptyDir volumes**
This namespace appears purpose-built to induce disk pressure on the node.

| Pod | Behavior | Estimated Disk Impact |
|-----|----------|-----------------------|
| `log-collector` | Continuously writes 256KB random files to `/var/log/app` every 5s (unbounded loop) | Unbounded; grows indefinitely |
| `nginx-cache` | Init container wrote 200× 1MB random files to `/tmp/nginx-cache` | ~200MB written at init |
| `postgres-emptydir` | PostgreSQL data stored on **emptyDir** at `/var/lib/pgsql/data` | DB growth on ephemeral storage |
| `redis-scratch` | `session-loader` sidecar writes 200× 512KB files to `/data` (AOF emptyDir) | ~100MB written by sidecar |

---

## Pod Details

### 1. `log-collector-66f67bf559-jfssx`
- **App/Role:** `log-collector` / `noise`
- **Owner:** ReplicaSet `log-collector-66f67bf559`
- **Container:** `logger` (busybox:1.36)
- **Command:** Infinite loop — writes 256KB random files to `/var/log/app/logfile-${i}.log` every 5 seconds via `dd if=/dev/urandom`
- **Resources:** Requests: 25m CPU / 32Mi RAM; Limits: 50m CPU / 64Mi RAM
- **Volume:** `emptyDir` mounted at `/var/log/app`
- **Status:** Running since 19:31:21Z, restartCount: 0
- **🔴 Risk:** Unbounded disk write loop — primary disk pressure contributor

---

### 2. `nginx-cache-79df9977c6-bnhxk`
- **App/Role:** `nginx-cache` / `noise`
- **Owner:** ReplicaSet `nginx-cache-79df9977c6`
- **Init Container:** `cache-warmup` (busybox:1.36)
  - Wrote 200× 1MB random files to `/tmp/nginx-cache` (1 per second, ~3min 20s)
  - **Completed** at 19:34:44Z (ran 19:31:21Z → 19:34:44Z), exitCode: 0
- **Main Container:** `nginx` (nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged:1.27-alpine)
  - Started: 19:34:45Z (after init completed), restartCount: 0
- **Resources:** nginx: 50m/64Mi req, 100m/128Mi limit
- **Volume:** `emptyDir` (`cache`) mounted at `/tmp/nginx-cache`
- **Status:** Running, all conditions True
- **🟡 Risk:** ~200MB pre-written to emptyDir at startup; static after init

---

### 3. `postgres-emptydir-66b7665c6d-h7vfd`
- **App:** `postgres-emptydir`
- **Owner:** ReplicaSet `postgres-emptydir-66b7665c6d`
- **Container:** `postgres` (quay.io/sclorg/postgresql-16-c9s)
- **Config:**
  - `POSTGRESQL_USER`: postgres
  - `POSTGRESQL_DATABASE`: postgres
  - `POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD`: from secret `postgres-credentials`
  - `PGDATA`: `/var/lib/pgsql/data/userdata`
- **Port:** 5432/TCP
- **Readiness Probe:** `pg_isready -U postgres` (delay 5s, period 10s)
- **Resources:** Requests: 100m CPU / 512Mi RAM; Limits: 500m CPU / 2Gi RAM
- **Volume:** `emptyDir` for DB data at `/var/lib/pgsql/data`; ConfigMap `postgres-init-sql` at `/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d`
- **Status:** Running, restartCount: **1**
  - **Previous termination:** exitCode 1 at 19:31:25Z (Error) — crashed on first start
  - Recovered and running since 19:31:27Z, Ready at 19:31:39Z
- **🔴 Risk:** Database stored on emptyDir (data loss on pod restart); had 1 crash on init; DB growth will consume node disk

---

### 4. `redis-scratch-645497ddcb-9bzkl`
- **App/Role:** `redis-scratch` / `noise`
- **Owner:** ReplicaSet `redis-scratch-645497ddcb`
- **Containers (2):**
  1. **`redis`** (redis:7-alpine)
     - Args: `--appendonly yes --dir /data` (AOF enabled on emptyDir)
     - Resources: 50m/64Mi req, 100m/128Mi limit
     - Running since 19:31:21Z, restartCount: 0
  2. **`session-loader`** (busybox:1.36) — sidecar
     - Writes 200× 512KB random files to `/data/aof-segment-${i}.dat` (every 2s), then sleeps
     - Resources: 10m/16Mi req, 50m/32Mi limit
     - Running since 19:31:21Z, restartCount: 0
- **Shared Volume:** `emptyDir` (`data`) mounted at `/data` by both containers
- **Status:** Running, all conditions True
- **🟡 Risk:** ~100MB written to shared emptyDir; AOF persistence on ephemeral storage

---

## Summary of Disk Pressure Causes

| Source | Type | Ongoing? |
|--------|------|----------|
| `log-collector` logger loop | Unbounded 256KB writes every 5s | ✅ Yes — critical |
| `nginx-cache` init warmup | 200MB one-time write | ❌ Complete |
| `postgres-emptydir` DB data | DB growth on emptyDir | ✅ Yes — grows with usage |
| `redis-scratch` session-loader | ~100MB write burst, then idle | ⚠️ Burst complete, Redis AOF ongoing |

**Root cause of node disk pressure:** All four pods store data exclusively on `emptyDir` volumes on node `dev-worker-0`, with `log-collector` providing a continuous unbounded write loop as the primary driver.

I now have all the data needed. Let me compile the full picture before submitting:

**Key Findings Summary:**

1. **Node filesystem** (`/dev/vda4` XFS, mountpoint `/var`): **63.2 GiB total, ~23.5 GiB free = ~36.8% available** — down from ~37.05% ~2.5 hours ago
2. **Trend**: Filesystem free space has been steadily declining: 37.05% → 36.83% over ~2h (a slow but consistent drain of ~0.22% per 2 hours on the reported filesystem axis, but with bigger drops visible around 19:17–19:27Z aligning
I'll start by setting up my investigation todos and discovering available action types simultaneously.

kaResponse.analysis: The demo-diskpressure namespace contains four deliberately disk-consuming workloads on node dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com, led by the log-collector Deployment running an unbounded 256KB write loop every 5 seconds with no log rotation, continuously draining the node's XFS /var filesystem (currently 36.8% free of 63.2 GiB).

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The demo-diskpressure namespace contains four deliberately disk-consuming workloads on node dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com, led by the log-collector Deployment running an unbounded 256KB write loop every 5 seconds with no log rotation, continuously draining the node's XFS /var filesystem (currently 36.8% free of 63.2 GiB).
causalChain: Signal: Predicted DiskPressure on node dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com — node's /var XFS filesystem at 36.8% free and declining
Why? Four pods in demo-diskpressure namespace are continuously writing data to emptyDir volumes backed by the node's /dev/vda4 partition

Why? The log-collector Deployment runs an infinite shell loop: 'dd if=/dev/urandom bs=256K count=1 of=/var/log/app/logfile-${i}.log' every 5 seconds, creating new files indefinitely with no deletion or rotation
Why? The Deployment container spec has no log rotation policy, no emptyDir sizeLimit, and no cleanup mechanism — the emptyDir volume grows without bound

Root cause: The log-collector Deployment is intentionally misconfigured (a 'noise' workload per its role label) to simulate disk pressure — its container command is an unbounded disk write loop with no storage limit, rotation, or garbage collection, designed to exhaust node disk space

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Remediation target is Deployment/log-collector in demo-diskpressure. This is the resource whose container spec (specifically the command and volume definition) must be changed to stop unbounded disk consumption. The Node is the symptom reporter, not the misconfigured resource. An automated workflow can modify the Deployment's container command or add an emptyDir sizeLimit.
dueDiligence.proportionality: log-collector is the primary driver (continuous unbounded writes) and highest-impact remediation target. The other workloads (nginx-cache static, postgres bounded by DB usage, redis-scratch burst complete) are secondary. Deleting or fixing log-collector addresses the dominant disk consumer. The namespace could also be deleted entirely if it is a test environment.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: all 4 pods in demo-diskpressure namespace, Deployment specs, node filesystem metrics (Prometheus), node events, container write rates. Did not investigate: other namespaces' disk usage (only demo-diskpressure was scoped), PVCs on other nodes, system-level disk consumers outside Kubernetes. The node filesystem trend is clear and dominated by identified workloads.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from DiskPressure signal → /var filesystem depletion → emptyDir volumes on /dev/vda4 → unbounded write loop in log-collector → missing rotation/sizeLimit in Deployment spec. Cannot go deeper: the Deployment spec IS the root configuration, and the workload is labeled 'role: noise' confirming intentional design.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims backed by tool output: (1) Pod listing confirmed 4 pods and their exact container commands; (2) Deployment YAML confirmed the infinite write loop command verbatim; (3) Prometheus node_filesystem_avail_bytes/size_bytes confirmed 36.8% free and total 63.2 GiB; (4) Prometheus range query confirmed downward trend over 2+ hours; (5) predict_linear confirmed ~23.4 GiB projected in 4h; (6) Node events confirmed kubelet restart at 19:30:54Z and NodeHasNoDiskPressure at same time. Inaccessible: Node describe (OpenShift RBAC Forbidden), direct kubectl_logs for logger container returned empty (pod running, low output at query time).
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/log-collector or Node/dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com per get_namespaced_resource_context and get_cluster_resource_context. N/A — no prior remediations to regress against.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Node-level issue unrelated to demo workloads — ruled out: all disk-consuming pods confirmed on this node, and trend correlates with pod deployment time; (2) Other namespaces contributing to disk pressure — not fully investigated but the /var filesystem hosts all pod emptyDirs cluster-wide; (3) Log rotation already in place — ruled out: Deployment spec confirmed no rotation command, no sizeLimit on emptyDir, and write loop generates unique filenames (logfile-${i}.log) so files never overwrite; (4) Prediction will not materialize — ruled out: predict_linear confirms continued decline, and mathematical projection (256KB × 12/min × 60min × 24h = ~4.3 GB/day) supports DiskPressure within days.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0. -0.05: Node describe returned Forbidden (could not verify current Node conditions/DiskPressure status field directly). -0.03: Logger container logs were empty (pod running but output at query time was blank — minor gap). -0.03: Did not quantify other namespaces' disk contribution to /var. Final: 0.89.

contributingFactors: log-collector Deployment: infinite loop writing 256KB random files to emptyDir every 5s (~3 MB/min, ~180 MB/hr) with no rotation or cleanup — files are never deleted
nginx-cache: init container pre-wrote ~200 MB of random files to emptyDir at startup (one-time burst, now static)
postgres-emptydir: PostgreSQL database stored on emptyDir (/dev/vda4), grows with DB usage
redis-scratch: session-loader sidecar wrote ~100 MB burst to emptyDir; Redis AOF persistence ongoing on same volume
All emptyDir volumes back to the same /dev/vda4 XFS partition (node /var filesystem)

Node's /var filesystem at only 36.8% free (~23.5 GiB) and declining — Prometheus range data confirms continuous downward trend from 37.05% to 36.83% over ~2 hours with accelerated drops at pod deployment time
Kubelet restarted at 19:30:54Z (NodeHasNoDiskPressure event confirms a clean start), but disk consumption resumes immediately with all 4 pods running
